subject: Cloud Computing
title: Colocation: Renting Rack Space
description: A quick, practical guide to Colocation: Renting Rack Space.

hook.text: In the late 1990s, companies shipped servers to data centers and paid for rack space.

buildup.text: Colocation meant you owned the hardware but rented the building, power, and cooling.

discovery.text: Managed hosting went further — the provider owned and maintained the servers for you.

twist.text: Both models had a fatal flaw: you guessed your capacity months in advance.

climax.text: Over-provision and you waste money. Under-provision and your site goes down.

punchline.text: Guessing capacity was a losing game. The cloud fixed it.

quiz.question: What was the key limitation of colocation and managed hosting?
options: Companies had to predict their server capacity needs far in advance
Data centers only existed in one country
Colocation was more expensive than owning a private data center
